title: The Arguments Against Open Source AI are Very Bad
excerpt: The release of Kimi K3 has opened a fresh round of angst and confused discourse. There's a loud cohort of journalists, business leaders, and politicians arguing that open source AI is a dangerous threat. OpenAI's Dean Ball:
content_html:
The release of Kimi K3 has opened a fresh round of angst and confused discourse. There's a loud cohort of journalists, business leaders, and politicians arguing that open source AI is a dangerous threat. OpenAI's Dean Ball:

One probable outcome of an open-weight-model-dominant world is full AI communism... rather than a market product, AI is a "public good"

Freely available AI for anyone? The horror!

Frontier labs' case against open source AI is essentially: Open source models1 are dangerous (and un-American!). We should open the AI Pandora's Box, but only with responsible gatekeepers (toll collectors, preferably us!). Only trusted users (our most profitable customers) should be able to use it.

I want to address some bad arguments against open source AI, but some corrections on how the argument is being framed are in order:

Open source software is the foundation for commercial software​

Ball's framing strolls past the fact that open source software is the foundation of all proprietary software. This includes frontier models, which at the end of the day are software products.

Open source software is counterintuitive to people outside of the software industry. Why work hard on a product, and give it away for free?

A software program is a stack of programs, with each layer built on top of another. To build Uber, you need programming language frameworks, software to send and receive web traffic, data analysis tools, and countless other components. Most of these are not differentiators for a commercial enterprise, so it serves commercial actors to cooperate on lower components in the stack and compete on the higher level pieces that actually differentiate their products.

Frontier labs would very much like AI models to not fall into the category of "so commonplace that it doesn't make sense to compete on". Whether that happens remains to be seen.

Open source software is very difficult to suppress​

In reality, the argument about suppressing open source models is mostly beside the point. History tells us that suppression of open source software is extremely difficult, and attempting to do so only serves to weaken companies against international competitors. A brief history of encryption is illustrative:

Today, PGP is a commonplace tool anyone can use, and most devs are at least familiar with. But when Phil Zimmermann invented it in 1991, the U.S. government considered encryption to be military technology. A criminal investigation was opened against Zimmermann.

When Netscape created SSL, the U.S. government allowed it to only release a weakened version of it internationally. These controls backfired: it was much easier to acquire the weakened, "international" version, so even many Americans used it.

Export controls did not succeed in limiting encryption as the government wished. SSL, PGP, and similar tools were readily available throughout the world, and the controls disadvantaged Americans. Eventually, courts ruled that releasing encryption source code is protected speech, and the U.S. government relaxed encryption export controls.

Narrowing suppression to "Chinese" models won't make things easier. What, exactly, makes an AI model Chinese? Is it Chinese if, as frontier models allege, it was distilled from American models? What about if an American fine-tunes a Chinese model? At best, regulating AI in this way will (temporarily) encumber Americans with red tape and diminished AI access relative to the rest of the world.

Open source AI is not just a Chinese phenomenon​

There's an assumption baked into the open source AI debate that open source models are something that only the Chinese government has an incentive to develop. In reality there are many commercial actors with ample incentive to develop open source AI:

  • Chip makers: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has described what Nvidia is building as “token factories”2. Nvidia doesn't care if its chips are used to run frontier models or cheap open source models3 - it just wants to produce and generate demand for as many tokens as possible. And indeed Nvidia has itself released a suite of open source models.
  • American Startups: Thinking Machines Lab recently released a powerful open source model. They and others are betting that models will be commoditized, and a defensible moat can be built around auxiliary services that complement or customize models.
  • Enterprise AI users: Frontier model customers aren't currently all that active in open source AI development, but they will be. They will want lower-cost models for low-complexity tasks, and more fine-grained control over customer-facing features.
  • BigCos: You can be sure that Google and Meta are watching OpenAI's new ad product closely. Should frontier model ad products gain traction, it would be well worth it for these behemoths to commoditize ad-free, open source models to squash ad competition.

The "AI race" is... what, exactly?​

Much of the angst around China's models centers on "losing the AI race". But what's the goal of this race? Is it to develop the best model? To sell the most tokens? To destroy humanity first?

Talking about an "AI Race" doesn't make more sense than talking about an "Internet Race". We're not competing to be the first to send a rocket to the moon, we're reacting to a new, transformational technology. To the extent there's a race between nations, it's to absorb this transition and grow economies. In this framing, free AI models are a boon, not a threat.

Bad arguments to fear Chinese AI models​

China is "AI dumping!"​

Scott Galloway has argued that free Chinese AI is an attempt to eliminate competitors in the long run:

This is what China did to solar panels, steel, EVs, and batteries. First, they match Western quality, or they don't even match it. 89%. Close. Actually, match it with cars, they've matched it, but go ahead. Then they cut the price by two thirds, then they own the market.

But apart from chips, AI isn't a physical good. Solar panels and steel require physical supply chains, each link of which cannot easily exist on its own. If no one is manufacturing solar panels in your country, it's difficult to build a business selling solar-grade silicon wafers.

Software isn't like that. An open source model coming from China doesn't prevent a fine-tuning business from succeeding in the US - quite the opposite!

They will spread propaganda!​

It's not unreasonable to assume that Chinese models will be shipped with a pro-China point of view. But this is not a reason to suppress them. The models are open source! If any American has an issue with the political slant of Chinese AI models, they are free to change and release an "Americanized" one. At least within the U.S., it's difficult to foresee a model seen as having a distorted pro-China bias outcompeting a substantially similar model with a distorted pro-U.S. bias.

They will add backdoors!​

AI does not change the basic market for vulnerabilities: responsible actors patch them, attackers exploit them. Limiting tools for responsible actors only serves attackers.

It's theoretically possible for a bad actor to embed hidden adversarial behavior in a model. But if this happens, it serves the interests of responsible actors to find these exploits as soon as possible, and the best way to do this is to let anyone who wants to inspect them.

Open source AI is coming​

It doesn't matter much what policy makers or business leaders want: open source AI is too powerful, and too difficult to control. It's coming, and attempts to squash it will not amount to anything more than noise along the way.

Footnotes​

  1. I'll use the terms "open source model" as in, "open weights model". ↩

  2. Quote taken from Derek Thompson's recent article on Chinese AI. Which, while we're here, gets a few things wrong:

    whoever is on the frontier is the best placed to dominate non-frontier markets as well, which are just the frontier minus n-months, i.e. months in which the frontier model makers have been optimizing their cost of serving.

    It's unclear why this should be the case. Their access to massive capital matters less for small-model development, and they lack incentive to do so rather than push users to their more expensive models.

    It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with

    Claude Code and Codex are sticky in the way that Coke and Pepsi are sticky: once you choose one, there's not much reason to switch. But this assumes similar cost and quality. In reality, coding agents have no moat. It takes a very small inconvenience to motivate users to switch agents, whether that be price difference, model quality, or reliability issues. ↩

  3. Ok, it cares a little - the ocean of capital going to train frontier models is certainly a good thing for Nvidia. But in the long run, if commercial token demand is replaced by demand for open source tokens, Nvidia still wins. ↩

sentences.en: The release of Kimi K3 has opened a fresh round of angst and confused discourse.
sentences.zh: Kimi K3的发布开启了新一轮焦虑和困惑的讨论。

sentences.en: There's a loud cohort of journalists, business leaders, and politicians arguing that open source AI is a dangerous threat.
sentences.zh: 许多记者、商界领袖和政界人士大声疾呼，开源人工智能是一种危险的威胁。

sentences.en: OpenAI's Dean Ball:
sentences.zh: OpenAI 的 Dean Ball：

blocks.html_en: One probable outcome of an open-weight-model-dominant world is full AI communism... rather than a market product, AI is a "public good"

sentences.en: One probable outcome of an open-weight-model-dominant world is full AI communism...
sentences.zh: 开放权重模型主导世界的一个可能结果是完全的人工智能共产主义……

sentences.en: rather than a market product, AI is a "public good"
sentences.zh: 人工智能不是市场产品，而是“公共物品”

sentences.zh: 人工智能不是市场产品，而是“公共物品”

blocks.html_en: Freely available AI for anyone? The horror!

sentences.en: Freely available AI for anyone?
sentences.zh: 任何人都可以免费使用人工智能吗？

sentences.en: The horror!
sentences.zh: 恐怖！

blocks.html_en: Frontier labs' case against open source AI is essentially: Open source models1 are dangerous (and un-American!). We should open the AI Pandora's Box, but only with responsible gatekeepers (toll collectors, preferably us!). Only trusted users (our most profitable customers) should be able to use it.

sentences.en: Frontier labs' case against open source AI is essentially: Open source models1 are dangerous (and un-American!).
sentences.zh: 前沿实验室反对开源人工智能的理由本质上是：开源模型1是危险的（而且不符合美国风格！）。

sentences.en: We should open the AI Pandora's Box, but only with responsible gatekeepers (toll collectors, preferably us!).
sentences.zh: 我们应该打开人工智能潘多拉魔盒，但只有负责任的看门人（收费员，最好是我们！）。

sentences.en: Only trusted users (our most profitable customers) should be able to use it.
sentences.zh: 只有值得信赖的用户（我们最赚钱的客户）才应该能够使用它。

blocks.html_en: I want to address some bad arguments against open source AI, but some corrections on how the argument is being framed are in order:

sentences.en: I want to address some bad arguments against open source AI, but some corrections on how the argument is being framed are in order:
sentences.zh: 我想解决一些反对开源人工智能的不良论点，但对这些论点的构建方式进行了一些更正：

blocks.html_en: Open source software is the foundation for commercial software​

sentences.en: Open source software is the foundation for commercial software​
sentences.zh: 开源软件是商业软件的基础​

blocks.html_en: Ball's framing strolls past the fact that open source software is the foundation of all proprietary software. This includes frontier models, which at the end of the day are software products.

sentences.en: Ball's framing strolls past the fact that open source software is the foundation of all proprietary software.
sentences.zh: Ball 的框架忽视了开源软件是所有专有软件的基础这一事实。

sentences.en: This includes frontier models, which at the end of the day are software products.
sentences.zh: 这包括前沿模型，它们归根结底都是软件产品。

blocks.html_en: Open source software is counterintuitive to people outside of the software industry. Why work hard on a product, and give it away for free?

sentences.en: Open source software is counterintuitive to people outside of the software industry.
sentences.zh: 对于软件行业以外的人来说，开源软件是违反直觉的。

sentences.en: Why work hard on a product, and give it away for free?
sentences.zh: 为什么要努力开发产品，然后免费赠送呢？

blocks.html_en: A software program is a stack of programs, with each layer built on top of another. To build Uber, you need programming language frameworks, software to send and receive web traffic, data analysis tools, and countless other components. Most of these are not differentiators for a commercial enterprise, so it serves commercial actors to cooperate on lower components in the stack and compete on the higher level pieces that actually differentiate their products.

sentences.en: A software program is a stack of programs, with each layer built on top of another.
sentences.zh: 软件程序是程序的堆栈，每一层都构建在另一层之上。

sentences.en: To build Uber, you need programming language frameworks, software to send and receive web traffic, data analysis tools, and countless other components.
sentences.zh: 要构建 Uber，您需要编程语言框架、发送和接收网络流量的软件、数据分析工具以及无数其他组件。

sentences.en: Most of these are not differentiators for a commercial enterprise, so it serves commercial actors to cooperate on lower components in the stack and compete on the higher level pieces that actually differentiate their products.
sentences.zh: 其中大多数都不是商业企业的差异化因素，因此它可以帮助商业参与者在堆栈中的较低组件上进行合作，并在真正差异化其产品的较高级别组件上进行竞争。

blocks.html_en: Frontier labs would very much like AI models to not fall into the category of "so commonplace that it doesn't make sense to compete on". Whether that happens remains to be seen.

sentences.en: Frontier labs would very much like AI models to not fall into the category of "so commonplace that it doesn't make sense to compete on".
sentences.zh: 前沿实验室非常希望人工智能模型不要落入“如此普遍以致于没有竞争意义”的类别。

sentences.en: Whether that happens remains to be seen.
sentences.zh: 这种情况是否会发生还有待观察。

sentences.en: Open source software is very difficult to suppress​
sentences.zh: 开源软件很难压制​

sentences.zh: 事实上，关于压制开源模型的争论大多是离题的。

sentences.en: History tells us that suppression of open source software is extremely difficult, and attempting to do so only serves to weaken companies against international competitors.
sentences.zh: 历史告诉我们，压制开源软件是极其困难的，试图这样做只会削弱公司对抗国际竞争对手的能力。

sentences.en: A brief history of encryption is illustrative:
sentences.zh: 加密的简史很能说明问题：

blocks.html_en: Today, PGP is a commonplace tool anyone can use, and most devs are at least familiar with. But when Phil Zimmermann invented it in 1991, the U.S. government considered encryption to be military technology. A criminal investigation was opened against Zimmermann.

sentences.en: Today, PGP is a commonplace tool anyone can use, and most devs are at least familiar with.
sentences.zh: 如今，PGP 是任何人都可以使用的常见工具，并且大多数开发人员至少都熟悉。

sentences.en: But when Phil Zimmermann invented it in 1991, the U.S. government considered encryption to be military technology.
sentences.zh: 但当菲尔·齐默尔曼 (Phil Zimmermann) 于 1991 年发明加密技术时，美国政府认为加密技术属于军事技术。

sentences.en: A criminal investigation was opened against Zimmermann.
sentences.zh: 对齐默尔曼展开刑事调查。

blocks.html_en: When Netscape created SSL, the U.S. government allowed it to only release a weakened version of it internationally. These controls backfired: it was much easier to acquire the weakened, "international" version, so even many Americans used it.

sentences.en: When Netscape created SSL, the U.S. government allowed it to only release a weakened version of it internationally.
sentences.zh: 当 Netscape 创建 SSL 时，美国政府只允许其在国际上发布其弱化版本。

sentences.en: These controls backfired: it was much easier to acquire the weakened, "international" version, so even many Americans used it.
sentences.zh: 这些控制措施适得其反：获得弱化的“国际”版本要容易得多，因此甚至许多美国人也使用它。

blocks.html_en: Export controls did not succeed in limiting encryption as the government wished. SSL, PGP, and similar tools were readily available throughout the world, and the controls disadvantaged Americans. Eventually, courts ruled that releasing encryption source code is protected speech, and the U.S. government relaxed encryption export controls.

sentences.en: Export controls did not succeed in limiting encryption as the government wished.
sentences.zh: 出口管制并没有像政府希望的那样成功地限制加密。

sentences.en: SSL, PGP, and similar tools were readily available throughout the world, and the controls disadvantaged Americans.
sentences.zh: SSL、PGP 和类似工具在世界各地都很容易获得，而这些控制措施对美国人不利。

sentences.en: Eventually, courts ruled that releasing encryption source code is protected speech, and the U.S. government relaxed encryption export controls.
sentences.zh: 最终，法院裁定发布加密源代码属于受保护的言论，美国政府放松了加密出口管制。

sentences.zh: 到底是什么让人工智能模型变得中国化？

sentences.zh: 如果正如前沿模型所声称的那样，它是从美国模型中提炼出来的，那么它是中国的吗？

sentences.en: What about if an American fine-tunes a Chinese model?
sentences.zh: 如果美国人对中国模式进行微调呢？

sentences.zh: 充其量，以这种方式监管人工智能只会（暂时）阻碍美国人的繁文缛节，并相对于世界其他地区减少人工智能的使用机会。

sentences.zh: 开源人工智能不仅仅是中国的现象​

sentences.en: Chip makers: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has described what Nvidia is building as “token factories”2.
sentences.zh: 芯片制造商：Nvidia 首席执行官黄仁勋 (Jensen Huang) 将 Nvidia 正在建设的工厂描述为“代币工厂”2。

sentences.en: Nvidia doesn't care if its chips are used to run frontier models or cheap open source models3 - it just wants to produce and generate demand for as many tokens as possible.
sentences.zh: 英伟达并不关心其芯片是用于运行前沿模型还是廉价的开源模型3 - 它只是想生产并产生对尽可能多的代币的需求。

sentences.en: And indeed Nvidia has itself released a suite of open source models.
sentences.zh: 事实上，英伟达本身也发布了一套开源模型。

blocks.html_en: • American Startups: Thinking Machines Lab recently released a powerful open source model. They and others are betting that models will be commoditized, and a defensible moat can be built around auxiliary services that complement or customize models.

sentences.en: American Startups: Thinking Machines Lab recently released a powerful open source model.
sentences.zh: 美国初创公司：Thinking Machines Lab 最近发布了一个强大的开源模型。

sentences.en: They and others are betting that models will be commoditized, and a defensible moat can be built around auxiliary services that complement or customize models.
sentences.zh: 他们和其他人押注模型将被商品化，并且可以围绕补充或定制模型的辅助服务建立防御护城河。

blocks.html_en: • Enterprise AI users: Frontier model customers aren't currently all that active in open source AI development, but they will be. They will want lower-cost models for low-complexity tasks, and more fine-grained control over customer-facing features.

sentences.en: Enterprise AI users: Frontier model customers aren't currently all that active in open source AI development, but they will be.
sentences.zh: 企业人工智能用户：前沿模型客户目前在开源人工智能开发方面并不那么活跃，但他们将会如此。

sentences.en: They will want lower-cost models for low-complexity tasks, and more fine-grained control over customer-facing features.
sentences.zh: 他们需要更低成本的模型来执行低复杂性的任务，以及对面向客户的功能进行更细粒度的控制。

blocks.html_en: • BigCos: You can be sure that Google and Meta are watching OpenAI's new ad product closely. Should frontier model ad products gain traction, it would be well worth it for these behemoths to commoditize ad-free, open source models to squash ad competition.

sentences.en: BigCos: You can be sure that Google and Meta are watching OpenAI's new ad product closely.
sentences.zh: BigCos：可以肯定的是，Google 和 Meta 正在密切关注 OpenAI 的新广告产品。

sentences.en: Should frontier model ad products gain traction, it would be well worth it for these behemoths to commoditize ad-free, open source models to squash ad competition.
sentences.zh: 如果前沿模型广告产品获得关注，那么这些巨头将无广告的开源模型商品化以压制广告竞争是非常值得的。

blocks.html_en: The "AI race" is... what, exactly?​

sentences.en: The "AI race" is...
sentences.zh: “人工智能竞赛”是......

sentences.en: what, exactly?​
sentences.zh: 到底是什么？​

blocks.html_en: Much of the angst around China's models centers on "losing the AI race". But what's the goal of this race? Is it to develop the best model? To sell the most tokens? To destroy humanity first?

sentences.en: Much of the angst around China's models centers on "losing the AI race".
sentences.zh: 围绕中国模式的大部分焦虑都集中在“输掉人工智能竞赛”上。

sentences.en: But what's the goal of this race?
sentences.zh: 但这场比赛的目标是什么？

sentences.en: Is it to develop the best model?
sentences.zh: 是为了开发最好的模型吗？

sentences.en: To sell the most tokens?
sentences.zh: 出售最多的代币？

sentences.en: To destroy humanity first?
sentences.zh: 先毁灭人类？

blocks.html_en: Talking about an "AI Race" doesn't make more sense than talking about an "Internet Race". We're not competing to be the first to send a rocket to the moon, we're reacting to a new, transformational technology. To the extent there's a race between nations, it's to absorb this transition and grow economies. In this framing, free AI models are a boon, not a threat.

sentences.en: Talking about an "AI Race" doesn't make more sense than talking about an "Internet Race".
sentences.zh: 谈论“人工智能竞赛”并不比谈论“互联网竞赛”更有意义。

sentences.en: We're not competing to be the first to send a rocket to the moon, we're reacting to a new, transformational technology.
sentences.zh: 我们并不是在竞争成为第一个将火箭送上月球的人，我们是在对一项新的、变革性的技术做出反应。

sentences.en: To the extent there's a race between nations, it's to absorb this transition and grow economies.
sentences.zh: 就国家之间的竞争而言，它是为了吸收这种转型并发展经济。

sentences.en: In this framing, free AI models are a boon, not a threat.
sentences.zh: 在这个框架中，免费的人工智能模型是一种福音，而不是一种威胁。

blocks.html_en: Bad arguments to fear Chinese AI models​

sentences.en: Bad arguments to fear Chinese AI models​
sentences.zh: 害怕中国人工智能模型的糟糕论据​

blocks.html_en: China is "AI dumping!"​

sentences.en: China is "AI dumping!"​
sentences.zh: 中国正在“人工智能倾销！”​

blocks.html_en: Scott Galloway has argued that free Chinese AI is an attempt to eliminate competitors in the long run:

sentences.en: Scott Galloway has argued that free Chinese AI is an attempt to eliminate competitors in the long run:
sentences.zh: 斯科特·加洛韦 (Scott Galloway) 认为，从长远来看，免费的中国人工智能是为了消除竞争对手：

blocks.html_en: This is what China did to solar panels, steel, EVs, and batteries. First, they match Western quality, or they don't even match it. 89%. Close. Actually, match it with cars, they've matched it, but go ahead. Then they cut the price by two thirds, then they own the market.

sentences.en: This is what China did to solar panels, steel, EVs, and batteries.
sentences.zh: 这就是中国对太阳能电池板、钢铁、电动汽车和电池所做的事情。

sentences.en: First, they match Western quality, or they don't even match it.
sentences.zh: 首先，它们符合西方品质，或者根本不符合西方品质。

sentences.en: Close.
sentences.zh: 关闭。

sentences.en: Actually, match it with cars, they've matched it, but go ahead.
sentences.zh: 实际上，与汽车相匹配，他们已经匹配了，但是继续吧。

sentences.en: Then they cut the price by two thirds, then they own the market.
sentences.zh: 然后他们将价格降低三分之二，然后他们就拥有了市场。

blocks.html_en: This is what China did to solar panels, steel, EVs, and batteries. First, they match Western quality, or they don't even match it. 89%. Close. Actually, match it with cars, they've matched it, but go ahead. Then they cut the price by two thirds, then they own the market.

sentences.en: This is what China did to solar panels, steel, EVs, and batteries.
sentences.zh: 这就是中国对太阳能电池板、钢铁、电动汽车和电池所做的事情。

sentences.en: First, they match Western quality, or they don't even match it.
sentences.zh: 首先，它们符合西方品质，或者根本不符合西方品质。

sentences.en: Close.
sentences.zh: 关闭。

sentences.en: Actually, match it with cars, they've matched it, but go ahead.
sentences.zh: 实际上，与汽车相匹配，他们已经匹配了，但是继续吧。

sentences.en: Then they cut the price by two thirds, then they own the market.
sentences.zh: 然后他们将价格降低三分之二，然后他们就拥有了市场。

blocks.html_en: But apart from chips, AI isn't a physical good. Solar panels and steel require physical supply chains, each link of which cannot easily exist on its own. If no one is manufacturing solar panels in your country, it's difficult to build a business selling solar-grade silicon wafers.

sentences.en: But apart from chips, AI isn't a physical good.
sentences.zh: 但除了芯片之外，人工智能并不是一种物理商品。

sentences.en: Solar panels and steel require physical supply chains, each link of which cannot easily exist on its own.
sentences.zh: 太阳能电池板和钢铁需要实体供应链，每个环节都无法轻易独立存在。

sentences.en: If no one is manufacturing solar panels in your country, it's difficult to build a business selling solar-grade silicon wafers.
sentences.zh: 如果您的国家没有人制造太阳能电池板，就很难建立销售太阳能级硅片的企业。

blocks.html_en: Software isn't like that. An open source model coming from China doesn't prevent a fine-tuning business from succeeding in the US - quite the opposite!

sentences.en: Software isn't like that.
sentences.zh: 软件不是这样的。

sentences.en: An open source model coming from China doesn't prevent a fine-tuning business from succeeding in the US - quite the opposite!
sentences.zh: 来自中国的开源模式并不妨碍微调业务在美国取得成功 - 恰恰相反！

blocks.html_en: They will spread propaganda!​

sentences.en: They will spread propaganda!​
sentences.zh: 他们会进行宣传！​

blocks.html_en: It's not unreasonable to assume that Chinese models will be shipped with a pro-China point of view. But this is not a reason to suppress them. The models are open source! If any American has an issue with the political slant of Chinese AI models, they are free to change and release an "Americanized" one. At least within the U.S., it's difficult to foresee a model seen as having a distorted pro-China bias outcompeting a substantially similar model with a distorted pro-U.S. bias.

sentences.en: It's not unreasonable to assume that Chinese models will be shipped with a pro-China point of view.
sentences.zh: 假设中国车型将带着亲中国的观点出货并不是没有道理的。

sentences.en: But this is not a reason to suppress them.
sentences.zh: 但这并不是打压他们的理由。

sentences.en: The models are open source!
sentences.zh: 这些模型是开源的！

sentences.en: If any American has an issue with the political slant of Chinese AI models, they are free to change and release an "Americanized" one.
sentences.zh: 如果任何美国人对中国人工智能模型的政治倾向有异议，他们可以自由地改变并发布“美国化”的模型。

sentences.en: At least within the U.S., it's difficult to foresee a model seen as having a distorted pro-China bias outcompeting a substantially similar model with a distorted pro-U.S. bias.
sentences.zh: 至少在美国国内，很难预见一个被视为具有扭曲的亲华偏见的模式能够胜过具有扭曲的亲美偏见的基本相似的模式。偏见。

blocks.html_en: They will add backdoors!​

sentences.en: They will add backdoors!​
sentences.zh: 他们会添加后门！​

blocks.html_en: AI does not change the basic market for vulnerabilities: responsible actors patch them, attackers exploit them. Limiting tools for responsible actors only serves attackers.

sentences.en: AI does not change the basic market for vulnerabilities: responsible actors patch them, attackers exploit them.
sentences.zh: 人工智能不会改变漏洞的基本市场：负责任的参与者修补它们，攻击者利用它们。

sentences.en: Limiting tools for responsible actors only serves attackers.
sentences.zh: 为负责任的参与者限制工具只为攻击者服务。

blocks.html_en: It's theoretically possible for a bad actor to embed hidden adversarial behavior in a model. But if this happens, it serves the interests of responsible actors to find these exploits as soon as possible, and the best way to do this is to let anyone who wants to inspect them.

sentences.en: It's theoretically possible for a bad actor to embed hidden adversarial behavior in a model.
sentences.zh: 从理论上讲，不良行为者有可能在模型中嵌入隐藏的对抗行为。

sentences.en: But if this happens, it serves the interests of responsible actors to find these exploits as soon as possible, and the best way to do this is to let anyone who wants to inspect them.
sentences.zh: 但如果发生这种情况，尽快发现这些漏洞符合负责任的行为者的利益，而做到这一点的最佳方法是让任何想要检查它们的人。

blocks.html_en: Open source AI is coming​

sentences.en: Open source AI is coming​
sentences.zh: 开源人工智能即将到来​

blocks.html_en: It doesn't matter much what policy makers or business leaders want: open source AI is too powerful, and too difficult to control. It's coming, and attempts to squash it will not amount to anything more than noise along the way.

sentences.en: It doesn't matter much what policy makers or business leaders want: open source AI is too powerful, and too difficult to control.
sentences.zh: 政策制定者或商业领袖想要什么并不重要：开源人工智能太强大了，而且太难控制。

sentences.en: It's coming, and attempts to squash it will not amount to anything more than noise along the way.
sentences.zh: 它即将到来，任何压制它的尝试都只会产生噪音。

blocks.html_en: Footnotes​

sentences.en: Footnotes​
sentences.zh: 脚注​

blocks.html_en: • I'll use the terms "open source model" as in, "open weights model". ↩

sentences.en: I'll use the terms "open source model" as in, "open weights model". ↩
sentences.zh: 我将使用术语“开源模型”，如“开放权重模型”。 ↩

blocks.html_en: I'll use the terms "open source model" as in, "open weights model". ↩

sentences.en: I'll use the terms "open source model" as in, "open weights model". ↩
sentences.zh: 我将使用术语“开源模型”，如“开放权重模型”。 ↩

blocks.html_en:
• Quote taken from Derek Thompson's recent article on Chinese AI. Which, while we're here, gets a few things wrong:

    whoever is on the frontier is the best placed to dominate non-frontier markets as well, which are just the frontier minus n-months, i.e. months in which the frontier model makers have been optimizing their cost of serving.

    It's unclear why this should be the case. Their access to massive capital matters less for small-model development, and they lack incentive to do so rather than push users to their more expensive models.

    It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with

    Claude Code and Codex are sticky in the way that Coke and Pepsi are sticky: once you choose one, there's not much reason to switch. But this assumes similar cost and quality. In reality, coding agents have no moat. It takes a very small inconvenience to motivate users to switch agents, whether that be price difference, model quality, or reliability issues. ↩

sentences.en: Quote taken from Derek Thompson's recent article on Chinese AI.
sentences.zh: 引自 Derek Thompson 最近关于中国人工智能的文章。

sentences.en: Which, while we're here, gets a few things wrong: whoever is on the frontier is the best placed to dominate non-frontier markets as well, which are just the frontier minus n-months, i.e. months in which the frontier model makers have been optimizing their cost of serving.
sentences.zh: 虽然我们在这里，但还是犯了一些错误：无论谁处于前沿，也最有可能主导非前沿市场，非前沿市场只是前沿减去n个月，即前沿模型制造商一直在优化其服务成本的几个月。

sentences.en: It's unclear why this should be the case.
sentences.zh: 目前还不清楚为什么会出现这种情况。

sentences.en: Their access to massive capital matters less for small-model development, and they lack incentive to do so rather than push users to their more expensive models.
sentences.zh: 他们获得大量资本对于小型模型开发来说不太重要，而且他们缺乏这样做的动力，而不是推动用户使用更昂贵的模型。

sentences.en: It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with Claude Code and Codex are sticky in the way that Coke and Pepsi are sticky: once you choose one, there's not much reason to switch.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，克劳德法典和法典的粘性程度令人震惊。无论您开始使用哪种安全带，都可能是您坚持使用的安全带。 Claude Code 和 Codex 的粘性就像可口可乐和百事可乐的粘性一样：一旦您选择了一种，就没有太多理由去切换。

sentences.en: But this assumes similar cost and quality.
sentences.zh: 但这假设成本和质量相似。

sentences.en: In reality, coding agents have no moat.
sentences.zh: 事实上，编码代理没有护城河。

sentences.en: It takes a very small inconvenience to motivate users to switch agents, whether that be price difference, model quality, or reliability issues. ↩
sentences.zh: 只要很小的不便就会促使用户更换代理商，无论是价格差异、模型质量还是可靠性问题。 ↩

blocks.html_en: It's unclear why this should be the case. Their access to massive capital matters less for small-model development, and they lack incentive to do so rather than push users to their more expensive models.

sentences.en: It's unclear why this should be the case.
sentences.zh: 目前还不清楚为什么会出现这种情况。

sentences.en: Their access to massive capital matters less for small-model development, and they lack incentive to do so rather than push users to their more expensive models.
sentences.zh: 他们获得大量资本对于小型模型开发来说不太重要，而且他们缺乏这样做的动力，而不是推动用户使用更昂贵的模型。

blocks.html_en: It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with

sentences.en: It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with
sentences.zh: 事实证明，克劳德法典和法典的粘性程度令人震惊。无论您开始使用哪种安全带，都可能是您坚持使用的安全带

blocks.html_en: It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with

sentences.en: It’s striking the extent to which Claude Code and Codex are proving to be quite sticky; whichever harness you start working with is likely to be the one you stick with
sentences.zh: 事实证明，克劳德法典和法典的粘性程度令人震惊。无论您开始使用哪种安全带，都可能是您坚持使用的安全带

In reality, coding agents have no moat. It takes a very small inconvenience to motivate users to switch agents, whether that be price difference, model quality, or reliability issues. ↩

sentences.en: Claude Code and Codex are sticky in the way that Coke and Pepsi are sticky: once you choose one, there's not much reason to switch.
sentences.zh: Claude Code 和 Codex 的粘性就像可口可乐和百事可乐一样：一旦你选择了其中一种，就没有太多理由去切换。

sentences.zh: 但这假设成本和质量相似。

sentences.zh: 事实上，编码代理没有护城河。

sentences.en: It takes a very small inconvenience to motivate users to switch agents, whether that be price difference, model quality, or reliability issues. ↩

blocks.html_en: • Ok, it cares a little - the ocean of capital going to train frontier models is certainly a good thing for Nvidia. But in the long run, if commercial token demand is replaced by demand for open source tokens, Nvidia still wins. ↩

sentences.en: Ok, it cares a little - the ocean of capital going to train frontier models is certainly a good thing for Nvidia.
sentences.zh: 好吧，它有点关心——用于训练前沿模型的资本海洋对于英伟达来说当然是一件好事。

sentences.en: But in the long run, if commercial token demand is replaced by demand for open source tokens, Nvidia still wins. ↩
sentences.zh: 但从长远来看，如果商业代币需求被开源代币需求取代，Nvidia 仍然会获胜。 ↩

blocks.html_en: Ok, it cares a little - the ocean of capital going to train frontier models is certainly a good thing for Nvidia. But in the long run, if commercial token demand is replaced by demand for open source tokens, Nvidia still wins. ↩

sentences.en: Ok, it cares a little - the ocean of capital going to train frontier models is certainly a good thing for Nvidia.
sentences.zh: 好吧，它有点关心——用于训练前沿模型的资本海洋对于英伟达来说当然是一件好事。

sentences.en: But in the long run, if commercial token demand is replaced by demand for open source tokens, Nvidia still wins. ↩
sentences.zh: 但从长远来看，如果商业代币需求被开源代币需求取代，Nvidia 仍然会获胜。 ↩

content_html_zh:
Kimi K3的发布开启了新一轮焦虑和困惑的讨论。 许多记者、商界领袖和政界人士大声疾呼，开源人工智能是一种危险的威胁。 OpenAI 的 Dean Ball：

开放权重模型主导世界的一个可能结果是完全的人工智能共产主义…… 人工智能不是市场产品，而是“公共物品”

任何人都可以免费使用人工智能吗？ 恐怖！

前沿实验室反对开源人工智能的理由本质上是：开源模型1是危险的（而且不符合美国风格！）。 我们应该打开人工智能潘多拉魔盒，但只有负责任的看门人（收费员，最好是我们！）。 只有值得信赖的用户（我们最赚钱的客户）才应该能够使用它。

我想解决一些反对开源人工智能的不良论点，但对这些论点的构建方式进行了一些更正：

开源软件是商业软件的基础​

Ball 的框架忽视了开源软件是所有专有软件的基础这一事实。 这包括前沿模型，它们归根结底都是软件产品。

对于软件行业以外的人来说，开源软件是违反直觉的。 为什么要努力开发产品，然后免费赠送呢？

软件程序是程序的堆栈，每一层都构建在另一层之上。 要构建 Uber，您需要编程语言框架、发送和接收网络流量的软件、数据分析工具以及无数其他组件。 其中大多数都不是商业企业的差异化因素，因此它可以帮助商业参与者在堆栈中的较低组件上进行合作，并在真正差异化其产品的较高级别组件上进行竞争。

前沿实验室非常希望人工智能模型不要落入“如此普遍以致于没有竞争意义”的类别。 这种情况是否会发生还有待观察。

开源软件很难压制​

事实上，关于压制开源模型的争论大多是离题的。 历史告诉我们，压制开源软件是极其困难的，试图这样做只会削弱公司对抗国际竞争对手的能力。 加密的简史很能说明问题：

如今，PGP 是任何人都可以使用的常见工具，并且大多数开发人员至少都熟悉。 但当菲尔·齐默尔曼 (Phil Zimmermann) 于 1991 年发明加密技术时，美国政府认为加密技术属于军事技术。 对齐默尔曼展开刑事调查。

当 Netscape 创建 SSL 时，美国政府只允许其在国际上发布其弱化版本。 这些控制措施适得其反：获得弱化的“国际”版本要容易得多，因此甚至许多美国人也使用它。

出口管制并没有像政府希望的那样成功地限制加密。 SSL、PGP 和类似工具在世界各地都很容易获得，而这些控制措施对美国人不利。 最终，法院裁定发布加密源代码属于受保护的言论，美国政府放松了加密出口管制。

将打压范围缩小到“中国”模式并不会让事情变得更容易。 到底是什么让人工智能模型变得中国化？ 如果正如前沿模型所声称的那样，它是从美国模型中提炼出来的，那么它是中国的吗？ 如果美国人对中国模式进行微调呢？ 充其量，以这种方式监管人工智能只会（暂时）阻碍美国人的繁文缛节，并相对于世界其他地区减少人工智能的使用机会。

开源人工智能不仅仅是中国的现象​

开源人工智能辩论中存在一个假设，即开源模型只有中国政府才有动力开发。 事实上，有许多商业参与者有足够的动力来开发开源人工智能：

  • 芯片制造商：Nvidia 首席执行官黄仁勋 (Jensen Huang) 将 Nvidia 正在建设的工厂描述为“代币工厂”2。 英伟达并不关心其芯片是用于运行前沿模型还是廉价的开源模型3 - 它只是想生产并产生对尽可能多的代币的需求。 事实上，英伟达本身也发布了一套开源模型。
  • 美国初创公司：Thinking Machines Lab 最近发布了一个强大的开源模型。 他们和其他人押注模型将被商品化，并且可以围绕补充或定制模型的辅助服务建立防御护城河。
  • 企业人工智能用户：前沿模型客户目前在开源人工智能开发方面并不那么活跃，但他们将会如此。 他们需要更低成本的模型来执行低复杂性的任务，以及对面向客户的功能进行更细粒度的控制。
  • BigCos：可以肯定的是，Google 和 Meta 正在密切关注 OpenAI 的新广告产品。 如果前沿模型广告产品获得关注，那么这些巨头将无广告的开源模型商品化以压制广告竞争是非常值得的。

“人工智能竞赛”是...... 到底是什么？​

围绕中国模式的大部分焦虑都集中在“输掉人工智能竞赛”上。 但这场比赛的目标是什么？ 是为了开发最好的模型吗？ 出售最多的代币？ 先毁灭人类？

谈论“人工智能竞赛”并不比谈论“互联网竞赛”更有意义。 我们并不是在竞争成为第一个将火箭送上月球的人，我们是在对一项新的、变革性的技术做出反应。 就国家之间的竞争而言，它是为了吸收这种转型并发展经济。 在这个框架中，免费的人工智能模型是一种福音，而不是一种威胁。

害怕中国人工智能模型的糟糕论据​

中国正在“人工智能倾销！”​

斯科特·加洛韦 (Scott Galloway) 认为，从长远来看，免费的中国人工智能是为了消除竞争对手：

这就是中国对太阳能电池板、钢铁、电动汽车和电池所做的事情。 首先，它们符合西方品质，或者根本不符合西方品质。 89%. 关闭。 实际上，与汽车相匹配，他们已经匹配了，但是继续吧。 然后他们将价格降低三分之二，然后他们就拥有了市场。

但除了芯片之外，人工智能并不是一种物理商品。 太阳能电池板和钢铁需要实体供应链，每个环节都无法轻易独立存在。 如果您的国家没有人制造太阳能电池板，就很难建立销售太阳能级硅片的企业。

软件不是这样的。 来自中国的开源模式并不妨碍微调业务在美国取得成功 - 恰恰相反！

他们会进行宣传！​

假设中国车型将带着亲中国的观点出货并不是没有道理的。 但这并不是打压他们的理由。 这些模型是开源的！ 如果任何美国人对中国人工智能模型的政治倾向有异议，他们可以自由地改变并发布“美国化”的模型。 至少在美国国内，很难预见一个被视为具有扭曲的亲华偏见的模式能够胜过具有扭曲的亲美偏见的基本相似的模式。偏见。

他们会添加后门！​

人工智能不会改变漏洞的基本市场：负责任的参与者修补它们，攻击者利用它们。 为负责任的参与者限制工具只为攻击者服务。

从理论上讲，不良行为者有可能在模型中嵌入隐藏的对抗行为。 但如果发生这种情况，尽快发现这些漏洞符合负责任的行为者的利益，而做到这一点的最佳方法是让任何想要检查它们的人。

开源人工智能即将到来​

政策制定者或商业领袖想要什么并不重要：开源人工智能太强大了，而且太难控制。 它即将到来，任何压制它的尝试都只会产生噪音。

脚注​

  1. 我将使用术语“开源模型”，如“开放权重模型”。 ↩
  2. 引自 Derek Thompson 最近关于中国人工智能的文章。 虽然我们在这里，但还是犯了一些错误：无论谁处于前沿，也最有可能主导非前沿市场，非前沿市场只是前沿减去n个月，即前沿模型制造商一直在优化其服务成本的几个月。 目前还不清楚为什么会出现这种情况。 他们获得大量资本对于小型模型开发来说不太重要，而且他们缺乏这样做的动力，而不是推动用户使用更昂贵的模型。 事实证明，克劳德法典和法典的粘性程度令人震惊。无论您开始使用哪种安全带，都可能是您坚持使用的安全带。 Claude Code 和 Codex 的粘性就像可口可乐和百事可乐的粘性一样：一旦您选择了一种，就没有太多理由去切换。 但这假设成本和质量相似。 事实上，编码代理没有护城河。 只要很小的不便就会促使用户更换代理商，无论是价格差异、模型质量还是可靠性问题。 ↩
  3. 好吧，它有点关心——用于训练前沿模型的资本海洋对于英伟达来说当然是一件好事。 但从长远来看，如果商业代币需求被开源代币需求取代，Nvidia 仍然会获胜。 ↩
